title: NOC 12010 – Supervisors, General Office and Administrative Support Workers | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors in office administration play a vital role in keeping businesses and public institutions running smoothly across Canada. Under NOC 12010, professionals who coordinate clerical teams, data-entry units, registry offices, and administrative operations enjoy strong labour demand and multiple pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Supervisors in this group may perform some or all of the following:
Coordinate, assign, and review the work of clerical and administrative staff
Oversee filing systems, records management, word processing, and data entry
Set work schedules and office procedures
Resolve operational issues and prepare progress reports
Train staff in duties, safety standards, and organizational policies
Ensure office equipment runs smoothly and arrange maintenance
employment_requirements: College-level coursework related to office administration or the area supervised
Prior experience in the occupation being supervised
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate 16,700 new positions for Supervisors, General Office and Administrative Support Workers, while around 14,300 job seekers may enter the market—creating favourable conditions for skilled newcomers.
additional_information: Supervisors in general office and administrative services remain in demand across both public and private sectors, including courts, hospitals, survey organizations, and corporate offices.
Why this occupation is attractive for immigration:

Consistent hiring nationwideStable supervisory roles with advancement potentialCompetitive compensationEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Strong long-term employment security
Typical annual salaries range from $36,900 to $89,900, depending on province, experience, and workplace setting.
Why Choose Canada as an Administrative Supervisor?

Access to universal public healthcareSafe and well-regulated workplacesExcellent work-life balanceA multicultural societyOpportunities to transition from temporary work permits to PRProvincial nomination streams targeting administrative and supervisory talent
